codeReview.summary: The current smart-push implementation now meets the feature contract. `ship()` auto-rebases when behind, aborts cleanly on rebase conflicts, classifies post-rebase push failures as `PushRejected`, establishes upstream tracking on first push, and returns accurate `commits_pushed` counts. The frontend push handlers in both `main-interface.tsx` and `mini-view.tsx` show the same 'Syncing with remote...' toast and matching conflict/partial-failure messaging. Follow-up fixes also added the required real git integration coverage for simple push, rebase-then-push, conflict abort, and no-remote scenarios.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The tauri-fullstack skill expects workers to add real smart-push workflow tests, but it does not document the temporary local repo + bare remote fixture pattern needed to do so. That omission likely contributed to the original smart-push worker only adding helper-level tests before the later follow-up test feature corrected coverage.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/tauri-fullstack/SKILL.md describes TDD expectations and even names `ship_simple_push` / `ship_rebase_when_behind` in its example handoff, but gives no guidance on building local bare-remote fixtures; the later fix feature `fix-ship-integration-tests` had to introduce that pattern in `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/git.rs`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared library still lacks any documented pattern for testing git CLI flows with temporary repositories and bare remotes, even though smart-push now depends on that approach for reliable validation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md documents command flow, settings flow, and AI provider behavior only; it does not mention git.rs integration-test fixtures or local bare-remote testing patterns now present in `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/git.rs` ship tests.

summary: Reviewed the smart-push feature handoff, transcript skeleton, current code, and the follow-up fix commits affecting it. Result: pass. The current implementation satisfies the requested smart-push behavior and now has matching frontend parity plus workflow-level Rust integration coverage for the critical push/rebase/conflict paths.
